tasks.description: Phase 1, Step 1: Add pendingOpsRef protection to resize in TimelineCanvas.tsx. In handleResizePointerDown (~line 501-535), increment pendingOpsRef.current after calling onActionResizeStart. In endResize (~line 476-499), decrement pendingOpsRef.current UNCONDITIONALLY (before the cancelled check) so both normal completion and cancellation decrement. Do NOT modify useClipResize.ts — the lifecycle is managed in TimelineCanvas. pendingOpsRef is already available via useTimelineEditorContext.
tasks.executor_notes: Added resize pending-op lifecycle in TimelineCanvas: increment after `onActionResizeStart` and decrement before the cancelled check in `endResize`, so both normal completion and pointer-cancel paths release the polling block. Verified in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and by the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description: Phase 1, Step 2: Protect useExternalDrop during async file uploads. Add pendingOpsRef to useExternalDrop's args interface. In handleFileDrop (~line 219-353), increment pendingOpsRef.current INSIDE the for-loop (~line 255) immediately before firing each async IIFE (~line 299). Decrement in a finally block inside each async IIFE. This ensures each in-flight upload independently blocks polling. Wire pendingOpsRef from useTimelineState.ts (~line 291-307) into useExternalDrop's args.
tasks.executor_notes: Threaded `pendingOpsRef` through `useExternalDrop` and `useTimelineState`, then moved upload blocking to per-file scope: each async upload increments inside the file loop before its IIFE and decrements in that IIFE's `finally` block. Updated hook test call sites and verified with the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description: Phase 1, Step 3: Audit and fix useAssetManagement concurrent drops. Check whether handleAssetDrop reads dataRef.current, mutates it to create a track, then calls applyEdit — and whether concurrent async calls can race on track creation. Fix by either folding track creation into a combined applyEdit payload (track+row+meta in one call) or using a synchronous dataRef guard (check dataRef.current for the track immediately before creating it, relying on JS single-threaded execution between awaits).
tasks.executor_notes: Audited `handleAssetDrop` and added an explicit synchronous re-check of `dataRef.current` immediately before creating a track. If another completed drop already materialized a compatible track, the later call now reuses it instead of creating a duplicate; otherwise it creates the track against the latest state. Verified in `useAssetManagement.ts` and by the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description: Phase 1, Step 4: Audit useTimelineHistory and useTimelineCommit to confirm undo/redo during save is safe. Verify that save-success reconciliation calls commitData with skipHistory:true, which means onBeforeCommit is never invoked during save reconciliation. Verify that undo calls restoreSnapshot → commitData with skipHistory:true. Confirm no race exists. This is audit-only — no code change expected. Document findings in executor_notes.
tasks.executor_notes: Audit confirmed no undo/redo vs save-history race. `useTimelineCommit` only invokes `onBeforeCommit` when `shouldSave && !skipHistory`. Save-success reconciliation in `useTimelinePersistence` commits with `save:false` and `skipHistory:true`. Undo/redo `restoreSnapshot` commits with `save:true` but `skipHistory:true`. `useTimelineState` wires `history.onBeforeCommit` into `save.onBeforeCommitRef`, so `skipHistory` prevents history re-entry during save reconciliation or undo/redo. No code changes were needed for this audit conclusion.
commands_run: rg -n "skipHistory|onBeforeCommitRef|onSaveSuccessRef|restoreSnapshot|commitData\(" reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts
cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description: Phase 3, Step 12: Create TimelineEventBus and replace 4 ref-wiring patterns. Create hooks/useTimelineEventBus.ts (~30 lines): Map<EventName, Set<Listener>> with on/off/emit. Four events: beforeCommit, pruneSelection, scheduleSave, saveSuccess. Migrate: (1) useTimelineCommit: accept bus, emit beforeCommit/pruneSelection/scheduleSave instead of calling refs. (2) useTimelinePersistence: accept bus, emit saveSuccess instead of calling ref. (3) useTimelineSave: create bus via useRef(new TimelineEventBus()), subscribe persistence.scheduleSave to scheduleSave event, pass bus to both useTimelineCommit and useTimelinePersistence. (4) useTimelineState: subscribe history.onBeforeCommit to beforeCommit, render.setRenderDirty to saveSuccess, pruneSelection callback to pruneSelection. Remove 4 refs + 3 useLayoutEffect wiring blocks.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a typed `TimelineEventBus` and migrated the four cross-hook ref bridges to event emission/subscription. `useTimelineCommit` now emits `beforeCommit`, `pruneSelection`, and `scheduleSave`; `useTimelinePersistence` emits `saveSuccess`; `useTimelineSave` owns the bus and subscribes `scheduleSave`; `useTimelineState` subscribes history, render-dirty, and selection-prune listeners from the bus. The old `onSaveSuccessRef`, `onBeforeCommitRef`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and `scheduleSaveRef` symbols are gone from the hooks layer, and the former wiring `useLayoutEffect` blocks were removed from `useTimelineState`, `useTimelineSave`, and the selection prune bridge. Verified by grep checks and by the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module passing with 23 test files / 82 tests.

watch_items: FLAG-001: Resize pendingOpsRef must decrement UNCONDITIONALLY in endResize (before cancelled check), not in onActionResizeEnd which is skipped on cancel
FLAG-002: External drop pendingOpsRef must be per-upload (inside for-loop + finally in each async IIFE), not per-drop (too shallow)
FLAG-003: Undo/redo during save is confirmed safe — skipHistory:true prevents onBeforeCommit during save reconciliation. Do NOT add unnecessary guards.

FLAG-004: The 4 refs to replace are onSaveSuccessRef, onBeforeCommitRef, pruneSelectionRef, scheduleSaveRef. onBeforeCommitRef and pruneSelectionRef live in useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave). scheduleSaveRef is created in useTimelineSave and wired to useTimelineCommit.
FLAG-005: Sub-contexts alone are insufficient — all 7 known consumers must be migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step, otherwise they still subscribe to the merged value
DEBT: agent-chat-undo-integration — agent chat edits bypass undo stack via skipHistory. Do not worsen this.
DEBT: registry-patch-testing — patchRegistry() lacks automated regression tests. Do not remove any existing test coverage.
DEBT: resolve-overlaps — resolve-overlaps.ts entirely lacks tests. Do not change resolve-overlaps behavior without adding coverage.
DEBT: selection-save-bridge — plan specifies passing pruneSelection into useTimelineSave but doesn't detail hook-layer bridge. The event bus (T12) must handle this.
Assumption: applyEdit is synchronous (updates dataRef.current immediately) — verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:152
Assumption: useDerivedTimeline is already clean (90 lines, pure useMemo) — verify in T16 before concluding no changes needed
Assumption: 2-way context split (Data + Ops) is target granularity, not 3-way (Data + Selection + Ops)
Test command: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/
All files in scope are under reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/

sense_checks.question: Does pendingOpsRef.current decrement happen BEFORE the cancelled check in endResize, ensuring both cancel and normal paths decrement?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `endResize` now decrements `pendingOpsRef.current` before the cancelled gate, so both cancelled and successful resize endings release the pending-op block.

sense_checks.question: Is pendingOpsRef incremented inside the for-loop (per file) and decremented in a finally block inside each async IIFE, not at the drop level?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `pendingOpsRef` increments once per file inside the `for` loop and decrements in the corresponding upload IIFE `finally` block, not once for the whole drop.

sense_checks.question: Does the fix prevent two concurrent async handleAssetDrop calls from both creating the same track? Is the guard synchronous (checking dataRef.current immediately before creation)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `handleAssetDrop` now re-reads `dataRef.current` immediately before track creation and reuses an already-created compatible track if one exists, preventing duplicate track creation across concurrent async completions.

sense_checks.question: Confirmed that commitData with skipHistory:true never invokes onBeforeCommit, so no undo/redo race exists during save? No code changes made?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `commitData` skips `onBeforeCommit` whenever `skipHistory:true` is set, and both save reconciliation and undo/redo restoration use `skipHistory:true`. This was audit-only; no code changes were made for T4.

sense_checks.question: Do all 71 tests pass after Phase 1 race condition fixes?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The full post-Phase-1 video-editor suite passed. The repository currently has 23 passing test files / 79 passing tests, so the plan’s 71-test count is outdated but fully covered by this run.

sense_checks.question: Is SortableRow wrapped in React.memo AND receiving per-clip resize override (not whole map)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `SortableRow` is memoized, and each row now receives only its own resize-override subset instead of the full override map.

sense_checks.question: Is ClipAction wrapped in React.memo with stable callback props (volatile values captured in refs if needed)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `ClipAction` is memoized, and the context-menu snapshot/open logic now reads volatile shot-action values from refs so the menu handler stays stable.

sense_checks.question: Was the resize preview re-render situation assessed? If T6 was sufficient, documented as no-op. If not, was ref+forceUpdate applied?
sense_checks.executor_note: Assessed and documented as a no-op. The T6 structure is sufficient: only the active row receives a changing resize-override subset, so a ref+forceUpdate path was not needed.

sense_checks.question: Is AudioTrack wrapped in React.memo with composite key pattern left intact?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `AudioTrack` is memoized and the composite key for Remotion remount behavior was left intact.

sense_checks.question: Do all 71 tests pass after Phase 2 rendering fixes?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The full post-Phase-2 video-editor suite passed. The repository currently has 23 passing test files / 82 passing tests, so the plan’s 71-test count is outdated but fully covered by this run.

sense_checks.question: Is useTimelineSelection a standalone hook (~100 lines) with clear interface, and useTimelineState reduced by ~100 lines?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `useTimelineSelection` now exists as a standalone hook with a clear explicit interface, and `useTimelineState.ts` was reduced from 523 lines to 397 lines by moving the selection slice out.

sense_checks.question: Are ALL 4 refs (onSaveSuccessRef, onBeforeCommitRef, pruneSelectionRef, scheduleSaveRef) replaced by event bus emissions? Are the 3 useLayoutEffect wiring blocks removed?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. All four ref bridges were replaced by event-bus emissions/subscriptions, and the old wiring `useLayoutEffect` blocks were removed from `useTimelineState`, `useTimelineSave`, and the selection prune bridge.

sense_checks.question: Is UseTimelineDataResult type removed? Do all downstream hooks accept specific args instead of the adapter shape?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `UseTimelineDataResult` is gone, the old adapter/object reassembly is gone, and the downstream hooks now consume specific typed args instead of the adapter shape.

sense_checks.question: Are there exactly 2 new sub-contexts (Data + Ops) with convenience hooks, and is the merged useTimelineEditorContext marked @deprecated?

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

1. **Phase 1 parallelism**: T1, T2, T3, T4 are independent and can be executed in parallel. T5 gates Phase 2.

2. **Phase 2 parallelism**: T6, T7, T9 are independent after T5. T8 depends on T6's result. T10 gates Phase 3.

3. **Phase 3 is strictly sequential**: T11 → T12 → T13 → T14 → T15 → T16. Each step builds on the previous. Run tests after each step within Phase 3 to catch breakage early, even though the plan only mandates tests at T17.

4. **Critical gotcha on T12 (event bus)**: The plan says scheduleSaveRef is created in useTimelineSave but called in useTimelineCommit. When migrating, the bus must be created in useTimelineSave (which orchestrates both useTimelineCommit and useTimelinePersistence) and passed down. Don't try to create the bus in useTimelineState — it must live at the useTimelineSave level where both consumers are composed.

5. **T13 may be partially obviated by T11**: After extracting selection, some of the dataHook adapter properties may already be gone. Read the actual code state after T12 before starting T13.

6. **T14 context split sizing**: The plan says ~30 data + ~35 ops props. Count the actual properties after T11-T13 — the numbers will have shifted. The split principle (read-only vs callbacks) is what matters, not the exact counts.

7. **T15 is the payoff step**: Without migrating consumers, the context split is pure overhead. If time is constrained, T14+T15 must ship together or not at all.

8. **Test strategy**: Run the full suite after every phase boundary (T5, T10, T17). Within Phase 3, run targeted tests after each step to catch issues early — don't wait until T17 to discover a broken import chain.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add pendingOpsRef protection to resize in TimelineCanvas
Step 2: Protect useExternalDrop during async file uploads
Step 4: Confirm undo/redo during save is safe (audit only)
Step 5: Run tests after race condition fixes
Step 8: Optimize resize preview to avoid state-driven re-renders
Step 10: Run tests after rendering fixes
Step 13: Eliminate the dataHook adapter object
Step 14: Split TimelineEditorContextValue into Data + Ops sub-contexts
Step 15: Migrate 7 consumers to sub-context hooks
Step 16: Slim useTimelineState with extracted concerns
Step 17: Confirm useDerivedTimeline is clean (audit only)
Step 18: Full test suite + new tests

validation.completeness_notes: All 19 plan steps mapped. Steps 16-17 combined into T16 (both are cleanup/audit). Steps 18-19 combined into T17 (verification phase). No gaps.
